question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R7 changes the IS-IS circuit-type on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (R7‚ÜîR6) to
`level-1`. R6's matching interface still operates as `level-2-only`
(inherited from the global is-type). The two ends now disagree on
level ‚Äî R7 sends L1 IIHs, R6 expects L2 IIHs. No adjacency forms. The
backup path R2‚ÜíR6‚ÜíR7‚ÜíR4 is broken; if the primary path R2‚ÜíR3‚ÜíR4 also
fails, traffic to R4 blackholes.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.107 (R7), the IS-IS circuit-type on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R7<->R6 link) was set to level-1 while R6's side operates as level-2-only (inherited from the global is-type).
The two ends now expect different IIH levels, so no adjacency forms across the link. The backup path R2->R6->R7->R4 is broken; if the primary R2->R3->R4 path also fails, traffic to R4 blackholes.
Fix: remove the circuit-type override on R7's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (return it to level-2).
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply ‚Äî silent scenario; mismatched
circuit-type silently prevents the adjacency from forming on the
next hello cycle, and the lab clock between revert and verifier
post-snapshot is short enough that no ADJCHANGE log lands in the
diff window. Diagnostic ground truth lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R7 `show running-config router isis LAB interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: `circuit-type level-1` line present (baseline: absent ‚Äî interface inherits global L2-only).
R7 `show isis interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 | inc Level|Circuit`: Circuit Type = Level-1 (other R7 interfaces still report Level-2).
R7 `show isis adjacency`: R6 no longer listed under Gi0/0/0/0 (baseline: R6 was a Level-2 adjacency).
R6 `show isis adjacency`: R7 missing on the corresponding Gi interface ‚Äî mismatched IIH levels prevent the adjacency from forming.
R2 `show isis fast-reroute summary`: backup coverage for the R2-R3 primary drops because the TI-LFA backup path R2‚ÜíR6‚ÜíR7‚ÜíR4 is broken (the R6-R7 link is no longer an adjacency).

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 circuit-type level-1

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no circuit-type
